text: Patient reports three positive home pregnancy tests taken over a two-day period
transcript_quote: Three positive home tests. I didn't trust the first one. Or, apparently, the second.

text: Approximately 10 weeks since last menstrual period (irregular cycles)
transcript_quote: when I got to about ten weeks since my last period

text: Patient is 43 years old (advanced maternal age)
transcript_quote: I'm forty-three, doctor.

text: History of prior early first-trimester pregnancy loss (miscarriage)
transcript_quote: I lost a pregnancy before, years ago, early on

text: History of hypertension since her thirties
transcript_quote: The blood pressure thing has followed me around since my thirties.

text: History of prediabetes
transcript_quote: High blood pressure. Prediabetes. Anemia.

text: History of anemia
transcript_quote: High blood pressure. Prediabetes. Anemia.

text: History of obesity and metabolic syndrome
transcript_quote: your weight has been in the range we classify as obesity, which along with the blood pressure and the blood sugar gets bundled into something called metabolic syndrome

text: Patient is not taking any regular medications (prescription, OTC, or herbal), including no antihypertensive therapy currently
transcript_quote: Nothing regular at the moment, no.

text: No vaginal bleeding or spotting since positive tests
transcript_quote: No bleeding, no cramps.

text: No cramping
transcript_quote: No bleeding, no cramps.

text: Reports breast/chest soreness
transcript_quote: My chest is sore

text: Reports afternoon fatigue
transcript_quote: I'm tired in the afternoons

text: Reports smell aversions (e.g., coffee)
transcript_quote: certain smells have become my enemies. Coffee, tragically.

text: Reports morning queasiness but keeps food down; no vomiting
transcript_quote: Queasy some mornings, but I keep food down.

text: No headaches, vision changes, or swelling of hands or face

slots.findings: no headache, vision changes, or edema

transcript_quote: None of that.

text: Increased urinary frequency but no dysuria (no burning)
transcript_quote: More often, sure, but no burning.

text: History of intimate partner violence by ex-husband; currently feels safe, remarried to a supportive partner
transcript_quote: My ex-husband. It was bad for a few years... But he's three states away, and I'm remarried now, and my husband is a gentle man. I'm safe.

text: Provided IPV resource card in patient packet
transcript_quote: I'll tuck a card into your packet, no strings attached.

text: In-office pregnancy test performed, confirming pregnancy
transcript_quote: We confirmed your pregnancy test here this morning.

text: Ultrasound viability scan performed showing intrauterine pregnancy with cardiac activity, appropriate location and growth
transcript_quote: That's the heartbeat, strong and steady, and everything is sitting exactly where it should be. This is a normal, healthy early pregnancy.

text: Fetal heart auscultated via Doppler with audible heartbeat
transcript_quote: we caught it on the Doppler too — that swishing sound you heard is exactly what we want

text: Uterine fundal height measured
transcript_quote: measure your belly for what's called fundal height

text: Physical examination performed
transcript_quote: I'll examine you

text: Blood group typing ordered/performed
transcript_quote: Blood type.

text: Complete blood count (hemogram) ordered to assess anemia
transcript_quote: A complete blood count, which also tells us where your anemia stands.

text: Hepatitis B surface antigen test ordered
transcript_quote: Screening for hepatitis B, hepatitis C, HIV, and syphilis.

text: Hepatitis C antibody test ordered
transcript_quote: Screening for hepatitis B, hepatitis C, HIV, and syphilis.

text: HIV test ordered
transcript_quote: Screening for hepatitis B, hepatitis C, HIV, and syphilis.

text: Syphilis test ordered
transcript_quote: Screening for hepatitis B, hepatitis C, HIV, and syphilis.

text: Rubella immunity screening ordered
transcript_quote: Immunity checks for rubella and chickenpox.

text: Varicella (chickenpox) immunity screening ordered
transcript_quote: Immunity checks for rubella and chickenpox.

text: Chlamydia swab/antigen test ordered
transcript_quote: Swabs for chlamydia and gonorrhea.

text: Gonorrhea test ordered
transcript_quote: Swabs for chlamydia and gonorrhea.

text: Urine culture ordered
transcript_quote: A urine culture, a urine check for protein, and a urine sugar screen

text: Urine qualitative protein test ordered
transcript_quote: a urine check for protein

text: Urine screening for diabetes/glucose ordered given prediabetes
transcript_quote: a urine sugar screen, given the prediabetes

text: Pap smear (cervical cytology) performed
transcript_quote: a Pap smear while we do the exam

text: Tuberculosis skin test placed on forearm, to be read in 2-3 days
transcript_quote: a small tuberculosis skin test on your forearm. Tiny prick, and we look at it again in two to three days.

text: Diagnosis of normal, healthy early intrauterine pregnancy
transcript_quote: This is a normal, healthy early pregnancy.

text: Follow-up prenatal visit scheduled in about 4 weeks, with monthly visits initially then more frequent later
transcript_quote: we'll see you in about four weeks

text: Counseled to call for warning signs: headaches, vision spots, sudden swelling of hands/face, given hypertension history
transcript_quote: I want you paying attention to headaches, spots in your vision, sudden swelling of the hands or face — those you call about, day or night

text: Counseled to call for bleeding, cramping, uncontrollable vomiting, or persistent worry
transcript_quote: you call us in between for anything at all — bleeding, cramping, vomiting you can't manage, or just a worry that won't quiet down

text: Nutritional counseling: regular meals, vegetables, iron-rich foods for anemia, gentle exercise (walking), and sleep
transcript_quote: regular meals, plenty of vegetables, iron-rich foods for the anemia — beans, greens, red meat if you eat it — and gentle movement. Walking counts. Sleep counts.

text: Optional genetic screening to be discussed at next visit
transcript_quote: at your next visit we'll sit down properly to talk about the optional genetic screening tests

text: Lab results to be delivered via patient portal
transcript_quote: the lab results will come to you through the portal as they finish

text: Family history of diabetes (mother diabetic in her fifties)
transcript_quote: My mother went diabetic in her fifties.

text: Counseled that closer/more frequent prenatal monitoring is warranted due to age and BP/blood sugar history
transcript_quote: means I'll be watching you more closely than the average patient — more visits, more checks
